⛵🌊

Happy Father's Day & Welcome, Summer!

June marks the official start of summer on the lake — the boats are back, the grill smells are drifting, and the fireflies are almost here. This is the very first edition of The Beacon, born from a simple idea: 88 neighbors across Harbour Pointe and The Harbours should know each other. Welcome aboard.

✍️ A Note from Your Editor

I'm Tony, living at 3321 Harbours right by the marina path. First off — hats off to our board for doing a killer job managing our community. Second: remember when we used to get neighborhood newsletters? Yeah, me neither. It's been that long.

Since I am not retired and apparently enjoy a glutton's portion of punishment, I decided to revive the tradition. Is this a new monthly, bi-monthly, or quarterly feature? Or is it a legendary "one and done" collector's item? Your guess is as good as mine.

A quick heads-up: I want to include both our immediate community and our neighbors at The Harbours, but any talk of rules and regulations applies strictly to Harbour Pointe on the Lake — the only bylaws I can legally quote.

⚖️

Legal fine print: I take zero responsibility for inaccurate facts. I am reporting the news as I know it — accuracy not guaranteed, but good intentions are. Hope you find this useful and enjoy the read!

— Tony  |  3321 Harbours Blvd

📢

Community News

⚓

Marina Boardwalk — Complete!

The marina project is in its final stages — the lights are in and looking great, with landscaping as the last remaining touch, set to get underway shortly. After a long wait, our waterfront is nearly ready to enjoy. More updates to follow as the finishing work is completed.

🔧 Nearing Completion 🌿 Landscaping Final Touch

🧱

The Harbours — Seawall Replacement

The Harbours is working to get their seawall and boardwalk replaced in the very near future — a significant improvement for our shared waterfront. As planning moves forward, there may be some activity near the marina area. We'll keep both communities informed on timing as details are confirmed.

📅 Coming Soon

🌿

Landscaping Underway

Kennedy Lawn & Snow (586-405-8980) is actively working the community. Report common-area concerns to Metro Group — not directly to crews. Sprinkler service: contact Rainman Sprinkler (248-721-0082).

📱

Sign Up for Text Alerts

Metro Group offers a text alert service for urgent association notices. Scan the QR code flyer in your welcome packet, or call 248-745-7100 to enroll. It's the fastest way to receive updates from management.

⚠️

Community Safety — Please Read

ℹ️

Note for Harbour Pointe on the Lake residents: The rules and policies listed in this section apply specifically to Harbour Pointe on the Lake as governed by its Master Deed, Bylaws, and Rules & Regulations. Residents of The Harbours should refer to their own governing documents, as rules may differ. When in doubt, contact your respective property management company.

🚗💨

Speeding — Our #1 Safety Issue

This reminder will appear every month until it stops being necessary
The community speed limit is 20 MPH — and it applies to everyone: residents, guests, delivery drivers, all of us. Our streets are shared with walkers, cyclists, children, and pets. Please slow down, remind your guests, and speak up if you see it happening. Our neighborhood's safety depends on every one of us.
🗑️

Trash Cans Belong in Your Garage — Always

Per the Handbook of Rules & Regulations — enforced by the Board
Trash receptacles must be stored inside your garage at all times — not on the driveway, beside the house, or in the yard. They may only be outside during pickup windows.

🗓 Trash day is Friday (Priority Waste · 855-927-8365). Place bags at the curb no earlier than 8:00 PM Thursday evening ✓, retrieve containers as soon as possible after collection. No pickup on major holidays — service runs one day late that week. Recycling bins out by 7 AM Friday.
🚘

Parking Rules (Harbour Pointe on the Lake)

No overnight street parking — ever. Park in garage or on your concrete apron. No commercial vehicles, RVs, or trailers stored outside (boat trailers: max 72 hrs on driveway). On cul-de-sacs: park on the condo side only. Guests use designated first-come areas.

🚒 During the day, please avoid parking directly opposite another parked vehicle — this narrows the road and can prevent fire trucks and emergency vehicles from getting through. It's a small courtesy that could make a big difference.

🐾

Pet Rules (Harbour Pointe on the Lake)

All pets must be leashed and attended at all times. "Keep your feet on the street" — walk pets only on your own lawn or the street. Use the sidewalk to reach the marina, not through neighbors' yards. Clean up immediately. Max: 3 pets of the same genus, no more than 5 pets total per unit.

🔨

Planning a Home Project? Board Approval Required Before Any Work Begins (Harbour Pointe on the Lake)

Any exterior alteration — deck, landscaping, paint colour change, driveway work — requires a completed Alteration or Modification Request Form submitted to Metro Group before work begins. The Board has up to 60 days to respond. Unapproved work may result in forced removal and fines. Only the 4 approved Sherwin Williams colour schemes and approved vinyl siding colours are permitted. Contact Metro Group for the form: 248-745-7100.

⚠️ Harbour Pointe on the Lake violation schedule — The Harbours residents: please refer to your own governing documents.

📋 Violation Schedule Consequence
1st ViolationWarning letter · no fine
2nd Violation$50 fine
3rd Violation$100 fine
4th & Beyond$200 per occurrence
🔥
Marina Fire Pit
Harbour Pointe on the Lake · Shared Amenity

The fire pit down at the marina is a gas fire pit — a great spot to gather on a summer evening and enjoy the water. A couple of important reminders and tips to keep it working well for everyone this season.

⚠️
Please — Gas Fire Pit Only

This is a gas fire pit, not a wood burner. Please do not throw trash, food, paper, wood, or anything else into it. Burning foreign materials damages the burner, creates smoke and odors for neighbors, and can be a safety hazard. Leave it as you found it so everyone can enjoy it.

🕹️ How to Light the Fire Pit
Step 1

Check the Emergency Shut-Off button — make sure it is not depressed. If it is, turn it clockwise to release it before proceeding.

Step 2

Turn the timer dial to your desired run time. Maximum is 2.5 hours — the fire will automatically shut off when the timer expires.

Step 3

Point the main dial arrow to the 12 o'clock position, then press and hold it down. Keep holding through Step 4, and continue holding for a count of 1–2–3–4–5 after Step 4 is complete, then let go and turn the dial all the way left to ignite.

Step 4

While still holding the dial from Step 3, press and hold the igniter button until you hear 3–4 clicks. You may need to press it several times. If the fire doesn't light, return to Step 3 — it sometimes takes a couple of attempts.

🔧 Troubleshooting
🔋

Igniter not clicking? The battery may be dead. Unscrew the igniter button — just like you would on your gas grill — and replace the battery inside. This is the most common fix.

🔥

Battery replaced and still no click? You can use a stick lighter as a backup — hold the flame near the burner while following Steps 3 and 4 above. Use caution and keep your hand clear when the gas engages.

📋
Board & Metro Group — Service Request

We'd like to kindly ask the Harbour Pointe on the Lake Board of Directors and Metro Group Management to arrange a service inspection of the marina fire pit before the height of summer. After a long winter, a professional check of the gas lines, igniter, and timer will ensure smooth, safe operation all season long for residents of both communities. Please contact Metro Group at 248-745-7100 or marti@mgmc.net to schedule.

🎟️

Neighbor Spotlight — Local Business

Ladle & Spoon
Homemade Soups, Salads & Baked Goods
Made with care. Delivered with love. 🤍
🏠 Your neighbor, Lia!
"Good food. Made with care. Delivered to you."
Your neighbor Lia runs Ladle & Spoon right here in the Waterford area — preparing fresh, scratch-made soups, salads, and baked goods every week and delivering them right to your door every Monday morning. No subscription, no commitment — order only when you want it. Perfect for busy families and seniors who want a warm homemade meal without the effort.
📋

New Menu

Posted every Tuesday — 3–5 soups & 1–2 salads to choose from

Senior Programs — Waterford Parks & Rec

🌟

Lifelong Recreation & Senior Services

Waterford Township Parks & Recreation · May/June 2026 · Leggett Campus · 248-618-7671
Waterford Township offers an incredible range of programs for seniors and older residents at the Leggett Campus — from fitness and dancing to card games, day trips, luncheons, and special events. Here are the highlights for May/June. Pick up the full newsletter at the Waterford Township offices (5200 Civic Center Drive) or call 248-618-7671.
🏓

Pickleball

Court shoes required. Mon, Tue, Wed & Fri. Drop in — $3 Resident | $4 Non-Resident. Punch card: 15 visits for $30 R / $40 NR.

🧘

Chair Yoga & Gentle Moves Yoga

Chair Yoga: Tuesdays 9:50 AM with Mari Ann. Gentle Moves Yoga: Thursdays 9:50 AM. Drop in or session registration. All levels welcome.

🕺

LaBlast Zumba & Line Dancing

LaBlast/Zumba: Mondays 6:30 PM. Country Line Dance: Tuesdays 10:10 AM (NEW!). Hustle Line Dance: Mondays 1 PM (NEW!). No experience needed!

🃏

Cards & Games

Euchre (all levels & competitive), Poker, Pinochle, Mexican Train Dominoes, Bridge, Bunco, Mahjong. Drop in from $2. Punch card: 20 visits for $40.

🎱

Shuffleboard & Table Tennis

Shuffleboard: Mon & Thu 10 AM–Noon. Table Tennis: Mon & Wed 10 AM–Noon. Drop in $3 R | $4 NR. Great low-impact fun!

🥎

60+ Men's Softball

Practices & games Mon & Wed, Clintonwood Park. Games at 10 AM. Season: May 4 – Mid August. $100 player fee. Contact Don Swanson: 248-674-1216.

🎨

Watercolour & Wood Burning

Watercolour: multiple sessions weekly. Wood Burning/Carving: Wednesdays 9 AM. Creative drop-in programs — bring your artistic side!

🚶

Walking Program

Daily walking sessions in the Gym. Mon/Wed/Fri: 8 AM & 3:45 PM. Tues/Thu: 3 PM. 20-punch walk card: $10 R | $12 NR. Feel free to bring headphones!

🤝 Services Available to Seniors

🚌
WOTA — Western Oakland Transportation Authority · 248-887-4979

Door-to-door demand-response transport for residents 18+ (priority to 55+, disabled). Mon–Fri 6 AM–9 PM · Sat 8 AM–4 PM · Sun 7:30 AM–3:30 AM · $2.00 per stop. Schedule 48 hrs in advance. All vehicles are lift-equipped.

📚
Outreach Home Delivery — Waterford Library · 248-674-4831

Homebound residents can receive free home delivery of audiobooks, large-print books, regular books, magazines, and DVDs. Contact Lynne Marozin at 248-618-7545 to see if you qualify.

🛂
US Passport Applications — Waterford Township Clerk's Office

5200 Civic Center Drive · 248-674-6266 · Appointment only. Photo service available on-site. Bring: Form DS-11, birth certificate, driver's licence, and payment.

Let's Build Community Together

What would you love to see happen in our neighborhood?

Our goal is simple: more connection, more pride, more reason to love living here on the water. Whether you're in Harbour Pointe or The Harbours — these are your ideas too. Talk to a neighbor, knock on a door, and help make one happen this summer.
